description: The BOUND StepContract + BoundCriteria the Cline dogfooding task is evaluated against. Field names match the installed bound package Pydantic models (bound.contracts.StepContract, bound.models.BoundCriteria) so a real Cline run can construct them directly from this JSON without renaming. The three required acceptance checks are identical to examples/agent_control_loop.py and the Cline integration prompt so the real trace is directly comparable to the deterministic example.

contract.description: Add robust input validation to the small API endpoint, including tests for valid, invalid, and edge-case input.
contract.goal: Add robust input validation to a small API endpoint.

acceptance_checks.description: Valid input is accepted and handled correctly.
Invalid input is rejected with an appropriate error response.
Edge-case input is handled without crashing.

risk_checks.description: No existing tests were removed to force a passing suite.
No unexpected files were created outside the task scope.

expected_artifacts: test file covering valid, invalid, and edge-case input

score_formula_note: With acceptance weight W_A=0.9 and three required checks, the weighted score S = W_A * (passed_required / total_required) lands at 0.3 / 0.6 / 0.9 for 1 / 2 / 3 checks passing, yielding REPLAN / RETRY / ACCEPT against T=0.7, retry_margin=0.2. No decision is hardcoded: these values are produced by the deterministic ContractEvaluator + BoundPolicy.
